_readme: Role → CLI routing for factory.js. Any CLI works. Placeholders: {PROMPT} is replaced with the assembled stage prompt.
io: 'stdout' = inputs are embedded in the prompt, stdout becomes the output file (works with ANY model/CLI, no tools needed).
io: 'tools'  = the agent is given file paths and writes the output itself (needs a tool-capable CLI: claude / hermes with hermes-cli toolset / codex).
parse: 'claude-json' only for `claude -p --output-format json` (unlocks cost tracking). Everything else: 'raw'.

Hermes headless is VERIFIED (2026-07-23): `hermes chat -Q -q {PROMPT} -m <model>` — stdout carries ONLY the final response (session id goes to stderr), so io:'stdout' roles work with any model Hermes can reach (OpenRouter/GLM/Kimi). See 'alternates' below for tested command shapes.
Promotion rule: no model enters a live role until it passes the model-bench packet (benchmarks/model-bench/) or a copy-gate/gauntlet-audited trial run. Alternates are staged, not hired.
The model-bench (benchmarks/model-bench/) is the hiring exam: before promoting a model into a role, run the packet, log the verdict in BUILD_REGISTRY, let the data decide.
Law 10 applies: quality at the LOWEST token cost that clears the gates. Director + critic are the only slots worth premium models.

research.label: research — synthesis over Black Book + interview

strategy.label: strategy — copy spine w/ VOC bindings

director.label: director — taste tier, runs once per client

copy.label: copy — strongest writer that benches clean

copygate.label: copy gate — fresh skeptic, different model than copy

build.label: build — executor tier; GLM-via-Hermes proven on Anchor v5

alternates._comment: Tested command shapes, staged for promotion after benching. Copy one over a role's cmd/args/io/parse to swap models.

hermes-glm.label: GLM via Hermes — verified headless 2026-07-23; Anchor v5 was GLM-built

hermes-tools.label: Hermes with file tools for io:'tools' roles — UNTESTED, verify --yolo/--accept-hooks behavior before live use
